translations.descricao: Heart failure (HF) is a clinical syndrome characterized by the heart's inability to pump sufficient blood to meet the body's metabolic demands, resulting in symptoms such as dyspnea, fatigue, and fluid retention. It is one of the main causes of global cardiovascular morbidity and mortality, with a high prevalence of risk factors such as hypertension and diabetes. In the Brazilian context, HF significantly affects the health system, with emphasis on integrated protocols for diagnosis and treatment.
translations.sus.indicacao: Diagnosis and treatment of HF recommended for patients with suggestive symptoms (dyspnea, edema) or risk factors such as hypertension and previous AMI, using integrated protocols in the SUS.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults with clinical suspicion of HF, including those with uncontrolled hypertension or history of AMI; prioritize vulnerable populations in primary care.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The AHA/ACC/HFSA (2022) recommends diagnostic evaluation for HF in patients with symptoms of congestion or reduced exercise tolerance, with stratification by ejection fraction.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of HF is approximately 1-2% in the adult population, increasing to >10% in elderly >70 years. In Mexico, high prevalence associated with risk factors. In Portugal, 15.2% of HFpEF in ≥50 years. In Brazil, estimated at 1.6-2.5% in the general population.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global incidence of 1-5 cases per 1,000 person-years, higher in populations with comorbidities. In Brazil, annual incidence of about 2-3 per 1,000 in adults >45 years.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: 5-year mortality of 50% for symptomatic HF. In Brazil, HF contributes to 10-15% of cardiovascular deaths, with a rate of 30-40 per 100,000 inhabitants.
